frameworks.name: visual cortical hierarchy
frameworks.one_line: Onset latencies should increase along the ventral/dorsal processing hierarchy.

frameworks.name: fast dorsal-stream routing
frameworks.one_line: Parietal cortex may receive visual input via shortcut pathways bypassing strict hierarchy.

core_question: How early do human posterior parietal regions respond to visual stimuli relative to peristriate visual cortex?
key_assumptions: ECoG onset latencies index local cortical processing
fMRI-defined visual area boundaries are valid landmarks for ECoG electrodes
Bootstrapped baseline thresholds yield reliable onset estimates
manipulations: Object category of presented images (excluding targets)
Electrode location across posterior cortical visual and parietal areas
key_findings: Onset latencies broadly followed the canonical visual hierarchy
A focal site on the posterior intraparietal sulcus responded around 60 ms
IPS onset was nearly simultaneous with peristriate visual responses
interpretations: Human posterior parietal cortex can engage as early as peristriate cortex, challenging strict feedforward timing
limitations_noted: Single epilepsy patient with right-hemisphere coverage only
